user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate 3 single-ended center-aligned 20 kHz PWM phases on TOM1 channels 2/4/6 with initial duties U=25%, V=50%, W=75%. Every 500 ms increase each duty by +10% and wrap within 0..100%, allowing true 0% and 100%. Apply synchronous shadow transfer for all channels. Update Cpu0 WAIT_TIME to 500 ms.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use unified IfxGtm_Pwm for the 3 PWM channels on TOM1 CH2/4/6 with synchronous updates. Provide a TOM1 CH0 timebase using IfxGtm_Tom_Timer with FXCLK0 to achieve 20 kHz center-aligned operation. Configure GTM clocks, map TOM TOUT pins, initialize PWM channels with requested initial duties, and at runtime update duties via IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDuty followed by IfxGtm_Tom_Timer_applyUpdate to commit shadow registers atomically.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified high-level PWM driver meets single-ended 3-channel requirement with simple init and provides updateChannelsDuty API for synchronous duty updates. Timebase explicitly provided by IfxGtm_Tom_Timer as requested.

runtime_function.name: GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM_update
runtime_function.purpose: Every 500 ms, increase U/V/W duties by +10% with wrap within 0..100% (inclusive), stage updates in PWM shadow registers, then commit synchronously via TOM timer shadow transfer.

driver_changes: Replace IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl with IfxGtm_Pwm for single-ended PWM channels
Add explicit timebase using IfxGtm_Tom_Timer on TOM1 CH0 with IfxGtm_Tom_Ch_ClkSrc_cmuFxclk0

api_changes.to: Center-aligned configured via IfxGtm_Pwm configuration tied to TOM1 CH0 timebase
IfxGtm_Tom_Timer_initConfig/IfxGtm_Tom_Timer_init for TOM1 CH0 @ FXCLK0 (20 kHz center-aligned)

migration_notes.context_preserved: User’s 3-phase, single-ended, center-aligned 20 kHz PWM on TOM1 CH2/4/6 is implemented using IfxGtm_Pwm with a TOM1 CH0 timebase. Initial duties are set to 25/50/75%. Duty updates are performed every 500 ms with +10% step and wrap in 0..100%, and synchronous shadow transfer is ensured by staging IfxGtm_Pwm updates then calling IfxGtm_Tom_Timer_applyUpdate. Dead-time and min pulse are omitted as requested. LS pins remain TBD per user policy.

PWM.rationale: Driver locked by refiner node (user explicit or engine-scored)
